title: Week 30
summary:
Capesize
A promising start to the week with sharp improvements in the Atlantic and steady rises in the East, but rates slipped as the week drew to a close with some aggressive numbers talked. Both fronthaul and transatlantic rates climbed, with the Puerto Bolivar/Rotterdam rate talked well into the $9.00s and fronthaul rates around $30,000 daily. As the week closed out a Cargill vessel fixed with GdF at $8.40 and suggesting something in the low $8.00 range has been agreed.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: A promising start to the week with sharp improvements in the Atlantic and steady rises in the East, but rates slipped as the week drew to a close with some aggressive numbers talked. Both fronthaul and transatlantic rates climbed, with the Puerto Bolivar/Rotterdam rate talked well into the $9.00s and fronthaul rates around $30,000 daily.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: The pace slowed from South America with many of the major charterers side-lined or talking the market lower, but equally many of the ships en route giving ETAs towards the end of August. There remained limited tonnage for early cargoes if they should appear.

children.title: Supramax / Handysize
children.summary: A busy week with a good amount of business concluded. However, as the last week of July approached some brokers feared the market is nearing the top while charterers held off fixing too far forward.
